title: Local drama group to debut play in Cape Town
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The Young Professionals drama group will be debuting their original play, Miss Morris, at the Theatre Arts stage in Observatory, Cape Town from 19 to 21 January.","html":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The Young Professionals drama group will be debuting their original play, Miss Morris, at the Theatre Arts stage in Observatory, Cape Town from 19 to 21 January."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The production team is made up of writer and director Samual Jumat and performers Lindie Strombeck, Michaela Whaites, Marveen Charles, Justin Thomas and Leonique Jacobs."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The actors, who have dedicated long hours to come together and put on a production that will see them debuting the play on a prominent stage which champions independent productions, are from Plettenberg Bay and Knysna.","html":"The actors, who have dedicated long hours to come together and put on a production that will see them debuting the play on a prominent stage which champions independent productions, are from Plettenberg Bay and Knysna."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"It is an absolute privilege to work with such aspiring, hard-working and well-committed actors who wear their hearts on their sleeve,\" says director Samuel Jumat.","html":"\"It is an absolute privilege to work with such aspiring, hard-working and well-committed actors who wear their hearts on their sleeve,\" says director Samuel Jumat."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Lindie Strombeck photographed for the production poster of Miss Morris.","html":"Lindie Strombeck photographed for the production poster of Miss Morris."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"It's a learning process, a tears and a goosebumps experience.\"","html":"\"It's a learning process, a tears and a goosebumps experience.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Miss Morris aims to evoke boundary-pushing contemplation of the educator-learner relationship. It digs deeper than just teaching as it highlights the role of teachers in local areas.","html":"Miss Morris aims to evoke boundary-pushing contemplation of the educator-learner relationship. It digs deeper than just teaching as it highlights the role of teachers in local areas."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This piece is based on a true story and written by the drama group's very own Samuel Jumat, who is a teacher himself."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The story emphasises how teachers' classroom lessons become life lessons, how today's teachers must be much more than just teachers and that 'it takes a village to raise a child'.","html":"The story emphasises how teachers' classroom lessons become life lessons, how today's teachers must be much more than just teachers and that 'it takes a village to raise a child'."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Tickets for the performance are available from the Theatre Arts website.","html":"Tickets for the performance are available from the Theatre Arts website."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Marveen Charles in a scene from Miss Morris. Photo: Moeniel Jacobs.","html":"Marveen Charles in a scene from Miss Morris. Photo: Moeniel Jacobs."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
